purpose: Separate list of non-correct Unknown Regions feats with description, proposed bucket/subbucket, expected shape, observed implementation, and accuracy concern.

entries.name: Acrobatic Ally
entries.description: You can hoist or toss an adjacent ally of your size or smaller up to 2 squares with a DC 20 Acrobatics check as a Standard Action, with shared initiative timing, ally positioning, prone-on-failure consequences, and possible attacks of opportunity.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Action workflow: validate adjacent ally, shared initiative count, size limit, DC 20 Acrobatics, destination square up to 2 squares above/away, AoO exposure, prone-on-failure, and turn-ending timing.
entries.observedImplementation: abilityMeta.actionRules describes ACROBATICALLY_MOVE_ADJACENT_ALLY with manualResolution. No proven GM/player action card, ally picker, square picker, shared-initiative validator, prone mutation, or AoO prompt exists in the uploaded baseline.
entries.accuracyFinding: Correct metadata shape, but not runtime-correct. It should remain partial until a coordinated movement action workflow exists.

entries.name: Acrobatic Dodge
entries.description: Once per encounter as a Reaction, if a melee attack misses you, immediately move into an adjacent square without provoking an attack of opportunity. Must be aware of the attacker; may use a second time by spending a Force Point.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Incoming missed melee attack reaction with awareness validation, adjacent-square movement picker, no-AoO movement, once-per-encounter tracking, and optional Force Point second use.
entries.observedImplementation: reactionRules metadata exists and old false numeric modifier is disabled. No proven incoming attack miss interception, reaction prompt, movement picker, encounter usage tracker, or Force Point second-use path exists.
entries.accuracyFinding: Correctly not a flat bonus, but only metadata/partial until reaction and movement runtime hooks are implemented.

entries.name: Combat Trickery
entries.description: Spend two successive Swift Actions on the same turn to make a Deception check against a target’s Will Defense. On success, the target is flat-footed against your next attack before the end of your next turn; a Force Point can extend penalties to the end of the encounter.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Action workflow: spend two swift actions, roll Deception vs Will Defense, apply next-attack flat-footed state with expiration, and optionally spend Force Point for longer duration.
entries.observedImplementation: skillUseRules metadata describes the behavior, but repository search only found skillUseSubstitutions and skillRerolls consumers, not a skillUseRules consumer. No two-swift action accounting, target Will comparison, flat-footed next-attack state, or Force Point duration extension was proven.
entries.accuracyFinding: Metadata is directionally correct but not runtime-correct.

entries.name: Elder's Knowledge
entries.description: Once per encounter, substitute a Knowledge (Galactic Lore) or Knowledge (Social Sciences) check for a Wisdom or Wisdom-related check.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Skill substitution prompt for Wisdom/Wisdom-related checks, limited to once per encounter, using Knowledge (Galactic Lore) or Knowledge (Social Sciences).
entries.observedImplementation: skillUseSubstitutions metadata exists and skill-feat-resolver reads skillUseSubstitutions, but once-per-encounter usage tracking and Wisdom-related check selection/prompt correctness are not proven.
entries.accuracyFinding: Likely close, but remains partial until encounter usage and eligible-check validation are verified.

entries.name: Frightening Cleave
entries.description: Immediately after using Cleave, each enemy within 6 squares and line of sight takes a stacking -1 penalty to Reflex Defense, attack rolls, and skill checks against you until the end of the encounter, stacking to -5. This is mind-affecting.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Post-Cleave trigger, affected-enemy target selection within 6 squares and line of sight, mind-affecting immunity handling, stacking debuff to -5 against the user, and encounter duration cleanup.
entries.observedImplementation: Current abilityMeta is gmManaged and archives rider rules. No proven Cleave trigger, area target acquisition, line-of-sight filtering, immunity handling, stacking effect application, or end-of-encounter lifecycle exists.
entries.accuracyFinding: Manual metadata is safe, but implementation is partial if the goal is runtime behavior.

entries.name: Grab Back
entries.description: Gain +2 Reflex Defense against Grab and Grapple attacks. If an enemy misses you with Grab or Grapple, immediately make your own Grab attack as a Reaction; if you have a qualifying grapple feat, you may make that Grapple attack instead.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Conditional +2 Reflex vs Grab/Grapple attacks plus missed Grab/Grapple reaction prompt that can use qualifying grapple feats.
entries.observedImplementation: grappleRules are consumed by grappling-system/grapple-legality-engine and reactionRules metadata exists. However, no proven incoming missed Grab/Grapple attack interception and reaction attack prompt exists.
entries.accuracyFinding: Defense/grapple metadata is likely partly supported, but the counterattack reaction is not proven, so not fully correct.

entries.name: Halt
entries.description: On a qualifying attack of opportunity with a weapon for which you have Weapon Focus, compare the same attack roll against the target’s Grapple check. On success, halt movement and knock prone; if the attack exceeds Damage Threshold, target loses remaining actions and ends its turn.
entries.expectedRuleShape: AoO-specific trigger with selected Weapon Focus validation, target size limit, same-roll Grapple comparison, prone/movement stop effects, DT check, and action-loss/turn-end rider.
entries.observedImplementation: Combat option metadata exists. No proven AoO event binding, selected Weapon Focus validation, same-roll Grapple comparison, target size validation, prone/movement mutation, or DT action-loss consumer exists.
entries.accuracyFinding: Correctly shaped as an attack-option/rider, but runtime correctness is not proven.

entries.name: Heavy Hitter
entries.description: With a Weapon Emplacement or Weapon System, add +1 damage for every 5 points your attack roll exceeds Reflex Defense. If you exceed Damage Threshold, the target cannot attack and speed is reduced on its next turn.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Vehicle/weapon-system attack context, calculate attack-margin damage after hit, and apply next-turn no-attack plus speed reduction if DT is exceeded.
entries.observedImplementation: rules metadata exists and combat-option-resolver can surface attack options. No proven damage formula consumer for attackMarginDividedBy5, weapon emplacement/system detection parity, or targetEffectsOnHit consumer for suppressing attacks and reducing speed was found.
entries.accuracyFinding: This is not fully correct until the damage and threshold rider are consumed by combat/vehicle runtime.

entries.name: Hold Together
entries.description: As a Reaction, spend a Force Point to delay the effect of damage dealt to a Colossal or smaller vehicle you are riding in or piloting until the end of the round.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Incoming vehicle damage interception, occupant/pilot validation, Colossal-or-smaller vehicle size check, Force Point spending, delayed damage queue, and end-of-round resolution.
entries.observedImplementation: actionRules metadata exists. No proven incoming vehicle damage interception, pilot/rider binding, vehicle size check, resource spend confirmation, delayed-damage queue, or end-of-round scheduler exists.

entries.name: Hyperblazer
entries.description: When traversing the Hyperspace Tangle, halve astrogation calculation time and penalties on Use Computer checks to Astrogate, and halve hyperspace mapping time for new routes.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Use Computer astrogation/hyperspace mapping workflow modifier for Hyperspace Tangle context, changing time and penalties rather than generic skill totals.
entries.observedImplementation: skillUseRules metadata exists, but no skillUseRules consumer or astrogation/hyperspace mapping subsystem consumer was found.
entries.accuracyFinding: Metadata-only/partial until astrogation/hyperspace navigation workflow consumes it.

entries.name: Improved Sleight of Hand
entries.description: Use Deception as a Swift Action before a Sleight of Hand Stealth check so observers must beat both your Deception and Stealth checks. Also draw and palm a weapon two sizes smaller as a single action with Stealth concealment handling.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Skill-use workflow that stores a Deception check, compares opposed Perception against both Deception and Stealth, and handles hidden weapon draw/palm/re-palm action timing.
entries.observedImplementation: skillUseRules metadata exists, but no skillUseRules consumer was found. No hidden-object/weapon palming state, opposed Perception dual-threshold, or action timing integration is proven.

entries.name: Improvised Weapon Mastery
entries.description: Treat all improvised weapons as Simple Weapons, use them without penalty and with feats/talents for Simple Weapons, and deal +1d6 damage on a successful hit.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Improvised weapon proficiency/no-penalty, simple-weapon feat/talent compatibility, and +1d6 damage with improvised weapons.
entries.observedImplementation: grants metadata appears to unlock improvised weapon proficiency, but ruleNotes explicitly leave +1d6 damage and feat/talent interaction GM-enforced. No damage formula consumer for improvised +1d6 was proven.
entries.accuracyFinding: Partially correct for proficiency; incomplete for damage and feat/talent compatibility.

entries.name: Intimidator
entries.description: When you successfully use Intimidate through Persuasion, instead of the normal result the target takes -5 to all skill checks, including Use the Force, and -2 to attacks while you are within line of sight until the end of your next turn; vehicle visibility/detection can satisfy the line of sight condition. Cannot be used with Maniacal Charge.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Persuasion/Intimidate success replacement workflow, target debuff application, line-of-sight/vehicle visibility condition, duration to end of next turn, and incompatibility with Maniacal Charge.
entries.observedImplementation: skillUseRules metadata exists, but no skillUseRules consumer was found. No target debuff application, visibility condition tracking, duration cleanup, or Maniacal Charge incompatibility enforcement is proven.

entries.name: Maniacal Charge
entries.description: When charging, make Persuasion checks as a Free Action to Intimidate the target and enemies passed within 1 square. Passed enemies that fail cannot make attacks of opportunity against you; target that fails loses Dexterity bonus and is flat-footed until the start of your next turn. Cannot be used with Intimidator.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Charge action option with path enemy detection, multiple Persuasion/Intimidate checks, AoO suppression from passed enemies, target denied Dex/flat-footed state, expiration, and incompatibility with Intimidator.
entries.observedImplementation: actionRules and ATTACK_OPTION metadata exist, but no proven path enemy detection, per-target Persuasion resolution, AoO suppression state, flat-footed target mutation, duration cleanup, or incompatibility enforcement exists.
entries.accuracyFinding: Correctly modeled as action metadata, but not runtime-correct.

entries.name: Nikto Survival
entries.description: Select a Nikto subspecies/native environment. In that advantageous native environment, reroll any Survival check and keep the better result.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Progression choice for Nikto subspecies/environment, context-gated Survival reroll in native environment, keep better result.
entries.observedImplementation: skillRerolls metadata exists and skill-feat-resolver reads skillRerolls. However, persistent subspecies selection/picker and native-environment context confirmation are not proven.
entries.accuracyFinding: Reroll hook is likely supported, but selected-choice and environment gating make it partial.

entries.name: Targeted Area
entries.description: When making a successful area attack with a weapon you are proficient with, select one target hit by the attack; that target takes +5 damage before Evasion is applied.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Area attack hit workflow with one-hit-target picker, proficiency validation, +5 damage to selected target before Evasion, and no global static damage bonus.
entries.observedImplementation: ATTACK_OPTION metadata exists and combat-option-resolver can surface requiresAreaAttack options. No proven selected target picker, per-target damage modification before Evasion, or proficiency validation specific to this rider exists.
entries.accuracyFinding: Close metadata shape, but not fully runtime-correct.

entries.name: Trample
entries.description: When using Mounted Charge, the mount makes a single natural weapon attack as a Free Action and compares the result to Reflex Defense of each enemy whose square it charges through; hit enemies take the mount’s natural weapon damage plus Strength modifier, and the normal end-of-charge attack still occurs. Either rider or mount may have the feat.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Mounted Charge path workflow with mount natural weapon attack, all crossed enemies as targets, Reflex comparison, natural weapon damage, normal end attack retained, and feat eligibility from rider or mount.
entries.observedImplementation: actionRules metadata exists. No proven mounted charge path detection, mount natural weapon roll/damage, crossed-square target collection, per-target Reflex comparison, or rider/mount feat inheritance consumer exists.
entries.accuracyFinding: Metadata only; mounted combat subsystem support is required.

entries.name: Wilderness First Aid
entries.description: Once per day, make a DC 20 Survival check. On success, when using Basic Survival, you count as having a medpac for Treat Injury checks until the end of the day; GM may adjust the DC by environment.
entries.expectedRuleShape: Once-per-day Survival DC check with environment-adjustable DC, success state until end of day, and Treat Injury medpac requirement waiver while using Basic Survival context.
entries.observedImplementation: skillUseAccess metadata exists, but repository search did not find a skillUseAccess consumer. No once-per-day tracking, Survival check workflow, environment DC override, or end-of-day state exists.
